title: Add bottom-right floating plus button to open PostTaskModal

requirements.summary: Add a fixed floating action button (FAB) in the bottom-right corner of the HubView that opens the PostTaskModal when clicked
acceptanceCriteria: A circular FAB with a '+' icon is fixed to the bottom-right corner of the HubView (e.g., bottom-6 right-6 with z-index above task cards).
Clicking the FAB opens the PostTaskModal.
The FAB uses the app's green-to-purple gradient consistent with other active/primary controls in the app.
The FAB is visible on both mobile and desktop layouts.
Any existing 'Post Task' button or trigger in the HubView that duplicates this action is either removed or left in place without conflict.

file_operations.description: Add a fixed-position circular FAB in the bottom-right corner (using Tailwind classes such as 'fixed bottom-6 right-6 z-50') that displays a '+' icon. Apply the app's green-to-purple gradient (bg-gradient-to-r from-primary to-secondary) to match the existing active button styles seen in CategorySelector and FilterBar. Wire the FAB's onClick handler to set isPostModalOpen to true, triggering the existing PostTaskModal. Ensure the FAB is positioned above all task cards and is visible on both mobile and desktop viewports. If the existing 'Post New Task' button in the top section creates UI redundancy, consider removing it or keeping both for different use cases (desktop vs mobile).
